perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in perpetually and upon request, Recipient will promptly return all data and materials furnished by LSUK , or provide written certification of its destruction and destroy any internal analyses and/or work papers related to these discussions .
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for one year from the Effective Date and may be renewed upon mutual agreement and upon request, Recipient will promptly return all data and materials furnished by LSUK , or provide written certification of its destruction and destroy any internal analyses and/or work papers related to these discussions .
perturbation.explanation: Changing the term of the agreement from 'perpetually' to 'one year' creates a direct contradiction. The agreement is now time-limited. However, the obligation to protect confidential information could be interpreted as surviving the termination of the agreement due to the nature of 'Confidential Information'. The original definition of confidential information created an in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: That all information, data, and materials furnished , either orally or otherwise by LSUK *specifically marked as confidential in writing* shall be considered "Confidential Information ," which includes all technical and non -technical info rmation concerning the past, present, and future business p ractices and/ or plans to include , without limitation, LSUK 's highly proprietary automated print on demand book manufacturing process , its processes, features, functions, performance, components, s ubsystems, use, technology (whether owned or licensed), intellectual property, resources, research, innovations, products or service offerings, strategic partners, techniques or processes, software, patent applications, inventions, trade secrets, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, marketing, strategies, or studies and all tangible and intangible property of any kind, whether conveyed in writing or orally by LSUK or its representatives to Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the agreement was perpetual. The changed text limits the agreement to one year. This directly contradicts the "Confidential Information" definition in the contradicted text. The contradicted text defines confidential information as information 'concerning the past, present, and future business practices', which implies that the confidentiality obligations continue beyond the one-year term of the agreement. Because the agreement now terminates after a year, and 'Confidential Information' covers information about 'future' business practices which would occur after termination, the changed text introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the scope and duration of confidentiality obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient recognizes the need for LSUK to disclose certain Confidential Information to be used solely for the purpose of evaluating any discussions in furtherance of this business relationship using an automa ted print on demand facility .
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient recognizes the need for LSUK to disclose certain Confidential Information to be used for the purpose of evaluating any discussions in furtherance of this business relationship using an automa ted print on demand facility , *but the recipient may retain any ideas gained during the tour*.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of '*but the recipient may retain any ideas gained during the tour*' creates a contradiction. The original clause implies that confidential information is only for evaluation. The new clause suggests the recipient can retain ideas (potentially derived from confidential information) for other purposes, undermining the non-disclosure aspect. This is an In-Text Contradiction to the rest of the contract which states Recipient agrees not to copy, duplicate, disclose or deliver all or any portion of the Confidential Information to a third party
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient agrees not to copy, duplicate, disclose or deliver all or any portion of the Confidential Information to a third party or permit any other third party to inspect, copy or duplicate the same except those parties deemed necessary by the undersi gned to evaluate a possible business relationship (including agents, advisors, affiliates, accountants, attorneys, consultants, and lenders). It is understood that the undersigned may disclose Confidential Information only to parties who (i) require such m aterial for the purpose related to this agreement and (ii) are informed by Recipient of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and agree to be bound by the terms hereof. The Recipient agrees to use its best efforts to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by parties in the preceding sentence, and that these parties will not use any of the Confidential Information for any reason or purpose other than to evaluate a possible business relationship and not in any way detrimental to LSUK . In the event a legal dispute arises concerning this Agreement, the prevailing Party *may* recover all court costs, expenses, and reasonable attorney's fees.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds '*but the recipient may retain any ideas gained during the tour*'. The original text says the confidential information is to be used solely for evaluation of the business relationship. However, the contradicted text states 'The Recipient agrees not to copy, duplicate, disclose or deliver all or any portion of the Confidential Information to a third party'. The change introduces a direct contradiction because the recipient retaining 'ideas' obtained during the tour implies they can use confidential information for purposes beyond evaluation, which contradicts the non-disclosure agreement. This aligns with the Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction definition where the change introduces ambiguity, making enforcement difficult. It also directly breaks the obligation in the contradicted section.
